postData.contentHtml:
The French Open has once again delivered a dose of high drama, with world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka suffering a shocking quarterfinal exit against Diana Shnaider. The defeat was so profound that a visibly distraught Sabalenka declared, “Just want to quit tennis right now,” following her 3-6, 7-5, 6-0 loss.

Sabalenka's path to victory seemed clear-cut, having secured the first set and establishing a commanding 4-1 lead with two breaks in the second. However, what followed was a complete and baffling collapse, as she lost 12 of the last 13 games. The highly experienced player attributed her performance to a combination of overthinking, unforced errors, and missed opportunities, drawing unfortunate parallels to her loss in last year's final against Coco Gauff.

The windy conditions on Court Philippe-Chatrier also drew Sabalenka's ire, as she voiced frustration over the decision to keep the roof open. “It was very dirty tennis. I don’t know how people could actually just sit there and watch me play,” she lamented, suggesting the elements played a role in her struggle.

News outlets echoed the sentiment of a dramatic upset, with headlines pointing to her "crumbling" and the "bizarre" nature of the defeat. Fellow tennis icon Venus Williams reportedly expressed empathy for Sabalenka's emotional and candid comments. On social media, reactions to Sabalenka's "quit tennis" statement have been largely positive, though some debate has emerged, reflecting a mix of support and discussion surrounding her raw emotional display.

Sabalenka's early exit is not an isolated incident in this year's French Open, which has proven to be a graveyard for top seeds. Defending champion Coco Gauff and four-time winner Iga Swiatek also tumbled out earlier than expected, setting the stage for an unprecedented tournament where, according to Opta, there isn't a former champion in either the men's or women's semifinals for the first time since 1977. This string of upsets underscores the unpredictable and fiercely competitive nature of this year's clay-court Grand Slam.
postData.title: French Open Shocker: Sabalenka's Heartbreak and 'Quit Tennis' Confession
postData.summary: Top-ranked Aryna Sabalenka faced a stunning defeat in the French Open quarterfinals, leading to an emotional outburst about quitting tennis. Her dramatic collapse against Diana Shnaider highlights a tournament full of unexpected upsets.
